metadata.title: Guideline on Non-Clinical and Clinical Development of Medicinal Products for the Treatment of Nausea and Vomiting Associated with Cancer Chemotherapy

rules.rule_text: This Guideline must be read in conjunction with Directive 2001/83/EC as amended and other relevant current and future EU and ICH guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: This Guideline intends to provide guidance for the evaluation of medicinal products for the prevention of nausea and vomiting associated with cancer chemotherapy and should be read in conjunction with Directive 2001/83/EC as amended and other relevant current and future EU and ICH guidelines.

rules.rule_text: Deviations from this guideline must be explained and discussed in the Clinical Overviews.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is guidance only, but deviations should be explained and discussed in the Clinical Overviews.
rules.exceptions: The guideline is guidance only, but any deviation requires justification.

rules.rule_text: Proof-of-concept studies in vivo must be performed in at least one species considered adequate for the proposed mechanism.
rules.verbatim_quote: Taking into account the limitations in the current understanding of the underlying mechanisms involved in the development of CINV, proof-of-concept studies in vivo should be performed in at least one species that is considered to be adequate for the proposed mechanism (see 2.2.1 below).

rules.rule_text: Different chemotherapeutic agents should be used to induce emesis to characterise possible differences in the pattern of activity.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is also encouraged to use different chemotherapeutic agents to induce emesis in order to characterise possible differences in the pattern of activity.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical models must be justified on the basis of good current scientific understanding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Models should be justified on the basis of good current scientific understanding.

rules.rule_text: Studies investigating site of action (central and/or peripheral) are encouraged and may include known centrally acting compounds as comparators.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies investigating site of action (central and/or peripheral) are encouraged and may, for example, include known centrally acting compounds (e.g. morphine, apomorphine).

rules.rule_text: The mechanism of action in relation to acute and delayed emesis must be described in sufficient detail to discern pharmacological targets, and the relative importance of peripheral and central target areas must be addressed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The mechanism of action in relation to 'acute' and 'delayed' emesis should be described in sufficient detail to discern pharmacological target(s), and, if possible, the relative importance of peripheral, e.g. gastrointestinal and central target areas, e.g. CTZ. Clinical relevance in relation to current science, and its biological properties in terms of class relationship to other molecules and known genetic variability should also be characterised.

rules.rule_text: The affinity/activity of the compound with the human target receptor must be characterised in vitro, and consideration must be given to the activity of corresponding animal homologues from the species used in in vivo studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The affinity/activity of the compound with the human target receptor should be characterised in vitro. Consideration should be given to the activity of the corresponding animal homologues from the species to be used in studies in vivo. An early receptor panel screen as general background information to address the specificity of the compound should be performed.

rules.rule_text: An early receptor panel screen must be performed as general background information to address the specificity of the compound.
rules.verbatim_quote: An early receptor panel screen as general background information to address the specificity of the compound should be performed.

rules.rule_text: Secondary pharmacology effects in the same species background strain as used for primary screening must be used to demonstrate the within-species potential for a therapeutic window.
rules.verbatim_quote: Secondary pharmacology effects in the same species background strain as used for primary screening should be used to demonstrate the within-species potential for a therapeutic window.

rules.rule_text: Unwanted effects on the CNS (e.g. sedation, motor incapacitation) must receive particular attention, especially for centrally acting compounds.
rules.verbatim_quote: unwanted effects on the CNS (e.g. sedation, motor incapacitation) may need particular attention, especially for centrally acting compounds.

rules.rule_text: The experimental basis for selection of first human dose must be clearly described, and the scientific basis for inclusion/exclusion criteria and any diagnostic markers used to select trial populations must be justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The experimental basis for selection of first human dose should be clearly described, and the scientific basis for inclusion/exclusion criteria and any diagnostic markers used to select the trial populations should be justified.

rules.rule_text: The possibility of a pharmacodynamic interaction between the anti-emetic drug and anti-tumour chemotherapy must be addressed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The possibility of a pharmacodynamic interaction between the anti-emetic drug and anti-tumour chemotherapy should be addressed.
rules.exceptions: Based on data and pharmacological considerations it might not be necessary further to study this issue in clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: For classes of compounds where non-clinical and clinical data are sparse, ex vivo cytotoxicity interaction studies in cancer cell lines supplementing studies in tumour bearing animals are recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: If not otherwise justified and especially for classes of compounds where non-clinical and clinical data are sparse, ex vivo cytotoxicity interaction studies in cancer cell lines supplementing studies in tumour bearing animals are recommended.

rules.rule_text: In vivo proof-of-concept models must use species in which emesis comparable to man occurs; the ferret, the dog, the house musk shrew (Suncus murinus), pigeon, rat, or pig are acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The ferret and the dog are the laboratory species most widely used to address the anti emetic activity of chemicals. Increasingly, the dog is replaced with the house musk shrew (Suncus murinus). Other species used are pigeon, the rat, or the pig.

rules.rule_text: In animal protocols, the test compound must generally be administered before the emetogenic challenge; cisplatin is the most frequently used emetogenic substance but studies using DTIC, cyclophosphamide, or ifosfamide are encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the test compound is administered before the emetogenic challenge. Cisplatin is the emetogenic substance most frequently used, but studies using other substances like, DTIC, cyclophosphamide, or ifosfamide are encouraged.

rules.rule_text: Antiemetic agents must be administered before the appearance of CINV; the goal of anti-emetic therapy is to prevent nausea and vomiting.
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be recognised that the goal of anti-emetic therapy is to prevent nausea and vomiting, and therefore antiemetic agents should be administered before the appearance of CINV.

rules.rule_text: Antiemetic agents must be used to prevent CINV in all patients receiving chemotherapy regimens of high and moderate emetic risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: According to current guidelines, antiemetic agents should be used to prevent CINV in all patients receiving chemotherapy regimens of high and moderate emetic risk.
rules.exceptions: No regular preventive use of antiemetics is suggested for patients treated with agents of minimal risk.

rules.rule_text: No regular preventive use of antiemetics is recommended for patients treated with chemotherapy agents of minimal emetic risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: No regular preventive use of antiemetics is suggested for patients treated with agents of minimal risk.
rules.exceptions: Applies only to minimal-risk chemotherapy agents.

rules.rule_text: Patients participating in efficacy studies must be well characterised with respect to covariates of importance for CINV, including demographic characteristics, Karnofsky performance status, and history of alcohol consumption.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients participating in efficacy studies should be well characterised with respect to covariates of importance for CINV, including the response to antiemetic therapy in previous chemotherapy cycles. In addition to demographic characteristics (age, sex, ethnicity), type of cancer (adjuvant, advanced/metastatic), Karnofsky performance status and history of alcohol consumption should be detailed.

rules.rule_text: Chemotherapy-naive patients must be studied and/or analysed separately from chemotherapy-experienced patients in efficacy studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chemotherapy-naïve patients should therefore be studied and/or analysed separately from chemotherapy-experienced patients. Those latter patients should be categorised into responders and non-responders to previous antiemetic treatment.

rules.rule_text: For documentation of treatment effects as regards acute CINV, a restricted number of predefined regimens falling within the same emetogenic risk category is acceptable from a regulatory perspective.
rules.verbatim_quote: From a regulatory perspective it is acceptable to use a restricted number of predefined regimens falling within the same emetogenic risk category for the documentation of the treatment effects as regards acute CINV.

rules.rule_text: For a non-restricted indication covering delayed CINV, activity must be documented separately against delayed CINV caused by both highly emetogenic cisplatin-based regimens and non-cisplatin-based regimens.
rules.verbatim_quote: For a non-restricted indication, it is currently recommended that activity should be documented separately against delayed CINV caused by highly emetogenic cisplatin and non-cisplatin based regimens. It is advisable to focus on one (or a few very similar) non-cisplatin based regimen with well-documented and clinically significant delayed CINV.
rules.exceptions: Non-clinical mechanistic data may be used to provide support for the possibility to extrapolate.

rules.rule_text: Emesis must be measured as the primary end-point by means of direct observation at the clinical site during first hours or, preferably, by means of a daily diary completed by the patient.
rules.verbatim_quote: Emesis has been the most frequently used primary end-point in studies with antiemetic agents. It can be measured by emetic episodes (vomiting and/or retches/dry heaves) by means of direct observation at the clinical site during first hours or, preferably, by means of a daily diary completed by the patient.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of nausea must include both duration and intensity measured by means of visual analogue scales or ordinal scores.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of nausea may be more difficult due to its subjective nature and nausea has been used as a secondary endpoint in many clinical trials. Both duration and intensity by means of visual analogue scales or ordinal scores should be assessed.
rules.exceptions: Nausea may be used as a secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Use of rescue medication or withdrawal must be considered as treatment failures in responder analyses.
rules.verbatim_quote: A patient may be defined as a responder if no emetic episode occurs. Control of nausea is usually less successful than control of vomiting. A patient may be considered as a responder if no, or only mild nausea is reported. Use of rescue medication or withdrawal should be considered as treatment failures.

rules.rule_text: The percentage of patients with Complete Control (CC) — defined as absence of emesis and nausea (or only mild) — is a required meaningful end point. No emesis and no use of rescue (R) is an alternative acceptable definition of response.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the relevance of both vomiting and nausea, the percentage of patients with complete control (CC), meaning absence of emesis and nausea (or only mild) is a meaningful end point. No emesis and no use of rescue constitute an alternative and acceptable definition of response (R).

rules.rule_text: Covariate information for known influential covariates in CINV must be prespecified and included in the statistical model; if chemotherapy-experienced patients are included, stratification by response to prior anti-emetic therapy must be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Covariate information for known influential covariates in CINV should be prespecified and included in the statistical model to control for potential imbalances (Points to Consider on Adjustment for baseline Covariates, CPMP/EWP/2863/99). If chemotherapy experienced patients are included, stratification by response to prior anti-emetic therapy should be considered.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy data are required for the whole risk period after receiving chemotherapy; the risk period for CINV after high and moderate emetic risk chemotherapy usually lasts 4–7 days.
rules.verbatim_quote: The risk period for CINV after receiving chemotherapy of high and moderate emetic risk usually lasts for 4-7 days and efficacy data are needed for the whole period at risk after receiving chemotherapy.
rules.exceptions: The period should be defined in relation to the administered chemotherapy regimen.

rules.rule_text: For main efficacy trials, CC or R during the first 24 hours (acute phase) or the full period at risk (e.g. 5 days) may be used as the primary endpoint; results for the full period at risk must be reported and at least non-inferiority demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Depending on the objectives of the study, CC or R during the first 24 hours after chemotherapy or for the full period at risk, e.g. 5 days may be used as primary end point for main efficacy trials. Also if the aim is to improve acute CINV, results for the full period at risk should be reported and at least non-inferiority demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: The efficacy of any product must be evaluated throughout multiple chemotherapy cycles.
rules.verbatim_quote: The efficacy of any product should be evaluated throughout multiple cycles.

rules.rule_text: Re-randomisation prior to the second cycle of chemotherapy must be considered in at least one confirmatory study, especially for new classes of compounds, to provide non-confounded data on sustained activity.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to provide non-confounded data on sustained activity, re-randomisation prior to the second cycle of chemotherapy should be considered in at least one confirmatory study. This especially refers to new classes of compounds.

rules.rule_text: When CC or R is selected as primary measure of efficacy, consistency is expected as regards secondary endpoints; when R is selected, control of nausea in particular must be addressed, accounting for the confounding effect of rescue medication use.
rules.source_section: 3.3 Methods to Assess Efficacy / 3.4.3 Main efficacy Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: If CC or R is selected as primary measure of efficacy, consistency is expected as regards secondary endpoints; in case of R especially control of nausea. The confounding effect of use of rescue, however, has to be taken into account.

rules.rule_text: If claims related to secondary endpoints are foreseen, a testing strategy must be in place avoiding multiplicity issues.
rules.verbatim_quote: If claims related to secondary endpoints are foreseen, a testing strategy should be in place avoiding multiplicity issues (CPMP Points to Consider on Multiplicity Issues in Clinical Trials, CPMP/EWP/908/99).

rules.rule_text: Pharmacokinetics of the experimental compound must be documented in accordance with relevant guidelines; initial PK studies must be conducted in healthy volunteers.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the pharmacokinetics of the experimental compound is documented in accordance with relevant guidelines. Initial PK studies should be conducted in healthy volunteers.

rules.rule_text: Specific drug interaction studies are expected if there is a mechanistic potential for interactions with cytotoxic compounds; PK and/or dynamic sampling must be carried out within efficacy/safety studies to provide further information on potential interactions.
rules.verbatim_quote: specific interaction studies are expected if there is a mechanistic potential for such interactions. In addition, it is recommended that sampling (PK and/or dynamic data, such as absolute neutrophil count of relevance for the cytotoxic compounds) is carried out within efficacy/safety studies in order to provide further information as regards potential or identified interactions.
rules.exceptions: The problems related to conducting interaction studies with cytotoxic compounds are recognised.

rules.rule_text: Dose and schedule finding studies must aim at defining a dose range and posologies where relevant clinical activity is shown; these studies must be parallel-group, fixed dose and schedule, double-blind controlled trials with at least 3 different dosages of the test treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to identify proper doses and posologies for confirmatory studies, dose and schedule finding studies should aim at defining a dose range and posologies where relevant clinical activity is shown. The design of these studies should be parallel-group, fixed dose and schedule, double-blind controlled trials with at least 3 different dosages of the test treatment.

rules.rule_text: Use of placebo alone is not recommended for exploratory studies in case of chemotherapy regimens with high or moderate emetogenic potential.
rules.verbatim_quote: Use of placebo alone is not recommended in case of chemotherapy regimens with high or moderate emetogenic potential.
rules.exceptions: Early monotherapy studies may be conducted in patients with regimens of rather low emetogenic potential in order to define active dosages.

rules.rule_text: The antiemetic efficacy of a new product must be evaluated in the first cycle and during subsequent treatment cycles to assess maintenance of effect; at least one trial applying re-randomisation prior to next cycle is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: The antiemetic efficacy of a new product should be evaluated in the first cycle and during subsequent treatment cycles in order to assess the maintenance of the effect. At least one trial applying re-randomisation prior to next cycle is recommended (see 3.2).

rules.rule_text: The reference product or regimen must be evidence-based and used in accordance with recognised and updated treatment guidelines taking into account the emetogenic risk both in the acute and the delayed phase.
rules.verbatim_quote: The reference product or regimen should be evidence-based and used in accordance with recognised and updated treatment guidelines taking into account the emetogenic risk both in the acute and the delayed phase.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials for main efficacy studies must be parallel-group, double-blind, randomised, and well controlled; for monotherapy studies, superiority or non-inferiority compared with an accepted active reference must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical trials should be parallel-group, double-blind, randomised and well controlled. With respect to monotherapy studies, superiority or non-inferiority compared with an accepted active reference should be demonstrated.
rules.exceptions: Non-inferiority studies must follow the Guideline on the Choice of the Non-Inferiority Margin (CPMP/EWP/2158/99).

rules.rule_text: A sufficient number of elderly patients must be included in confirmatory studies; conduct of studies that include patients above age 75 is encouraged; paediatric studies programme outlines must be available at time of marketing approval submission.
rules.verbatim_quote: In accordance with general guidelines and especially as cancer is a disease of the elderly, a sufficient number of elderly patients should be included in the confirmatory studies to provide a firm basis for the assessment of safety and efficacy. The conduct of studies that include patients above the age of 75 is encouraged. The outlines of a paediatric studies programme should be available at the time of submission for marketing approval.
